RE 3 Request f or Advice lJetter
Dear Hallabrin:
This office serves as the city Attorney for the City of
Dublin. New1y elected City Councilmember Houston has requested
an advice letter as set forth below.
BACKGROUND
Guy Houston is a newly elected councilmember in the City of
Dublin. The City Council is scheduled to consider a general plan
amendment and specific plan for the Eastern Dub1in area (the rrGPA
Decisiontt) on January 12, 1993. The area encompassed in the GPA
Decision is approximately 7,OOO acres. As presently drafted, the
GPA would provide for developnent of 18,000 new dwelling units,
with complete buildout over a 20 to 30 year period. The General
plan Amendment and Specific PIan for the Eastern Dublin area is
sponsored by the City, not a developer. There are approximately
50 property owners in the 7,000 acre area-
Councilmember Houston is a limited partner in Valley
Capital, a mortgage brokering business. Virtually all of Val1ey
Capitatts business concerns existing homes, not new homes.
Councilrnember Houston has only generated one mortgage on a new
home which was not located in Dublin. Approximately 58 of Va11ey
Capitalrs business is within the City of Dublin, with the
reiraining 952 of its business being conducted outside the City's
boundaries. More specifically, approximately l2e" of
Councilmernber Houstonts business is within the City of Dublin,
with the remainder conducted outside the City. 
Finally,
approximately Seo of VaIleyts business involves ttlumborr loans


Scott Haltabrin
December 22, 7992
Page 2
(Ioans over $202,3oo in amount), and the average foan amount of
valley's business is $160, 00o.
Valley capital receives a commission for mortgages which it
brokers. Commissions are measured as 1t to L|1Z of the loan
amount. As a I j,nited partner, councj-l,member Houston receives 808
of those comrnissions which he generates, lrith the remaining 20?
going to the business.
raauEa
May councilmember Houston participate in the city Council
cPA Decision on January 12, L993?
We befieve uncertainty exists as to any foreseeable
financial effect, given the fact that the GPA Decision wifl
result in the construction of new homes, while Va1ley Capitalrs
business focuses almost exclusively on brokering mortgages for
existing, not new, homes. Furthermore, only a very small
percentage of val-l-ey capital and councilmember Houstonrs business
is conducted within the city Iimits. 
we believe there are also
issues concerning rnaterial financial effects of the GPA Decision.
As mentioned above, the council, has scheduled the GPA
Decision for its January 12, 1993 Councj-1, meeting. We eroul-d
appreciate resolution of this issue prior to this date. If you
have any questions concerning this or need additional informa-
tion, please do not hesitate to contact me.
very truly yours,
I.{EYERS , NAVE, RIBACK & SILVER
{rl'ruV.tk -
Elizabeth H. Silver
city Attorney
City of Dublin

sections.question: May councilmember Houston participate in the City Council GPA Decision on January 12, 1993?

sections.question_synthetic: Can Councilmember Houston, a limited partner in a mortgage brokering business that primarily handles existing home loans with minimal activity in Dublin, participate in voting on a general plan amendment for development of 18,000 new dwelling units in Eastern Dublin?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from city attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Contains background section and issue/question section but no conclusion section from FPPC. Document seeks advance ruling before January 12, 1993 council meeting.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney for Dublin requests an FPPC advice letter regarding whether newly elected Councilmember Houston may participate in voting on a general plan amendment for Eastern Dublin, given his financial interest as a limited partner in Valley Capital, a mortgage brokering business.
